titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Edmund Haynes Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11222654.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 05:08

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The George Edmund Haynes Papers consist of correspondence, writings, and professional papers by and relating to George Edmund Haynes. Correspondence contains letters between Haynes and various professional groups that he worked for or with. Writings contain works by Haynes, including articles and book reviews; and a dissertation on Haynes by Daniel Perlman. Professional papers contain material relating to Haynes' professional work on various committees and boards, including the Federal Council of Churches, the World's Committee of YMCAs, the State University of New York Board of Trustees, and a survey of the work of the YMCA in South Africa.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of George Edmund Haynes, 1953-1960 and Olyve Haynes, 1960-1972.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The George Edmund Haynes Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Edmund Haynes Papers. James Weldon Johnson Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as Za JWJ MSS Haynes, George.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Portions of the collection are also available on microfilm #1292. See folder labels for more information.

bioghist.head: George Edmund Haynes (1880-1960)
bioghist.p: George Edmund Haynes, American social worker, educator, and co-founder and first executive director of the National Urban League.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The George Edmund Haynes Papers consist of correspondence, writings, and professional papers by and relating to George Edmund Haynes. Correspondence contains letters between Haynes and various professional groups that he worked for or with. Writings contain works by Haynes, including articles and book reviews; and a dissertation on Haynes by Daniel Perlman. Professional papers contain material relating to Haynes' professional work on various committees and boards, including the Federal Council of Churches, the World's Committee of YMCAs, the State University of New York Board of Trustees, and a survey of the work of the YMCA in South Africa.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1916-1944. II. Writings, 1929-1950, 1972. III. Professional Papers, 1928-1949.

corpname.part: Federal Council of the Churches of Christ in America
State University of New York. Board of Trustees
World's Committee of Young Men's Christian Associations

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent. Correspondence with local and state interracial committees and various correspondence is filed at the end of the series.

did.unittitle: Federal Council of Churches, African Welfare Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Haynes and educational religious contacts for Africa

did.unittitle: New York State University Temporary Commission

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings by Haynes, including articles and book reviews. A dissertation on Haynes by Daniel Perlman is filed at the end of the series.

did.unittitle: "Africa, Continent of the Future," original typescript and typescript carbon drafts
"Negroes," article for the Social Work Year Book, typescript draft, corrected
Various articles, original typescript and typescript carbon drafts
Book reviews, original typescript and typescript drafts
"Stirring White Conscience: The Life of George Edmund Haynes" by Daniel Perlman, typescript (photocopy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dissertation in the Department of History, Graduate School of Arts and Sciences, New York University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains papers relating to Haynes' professional work on various committees and boards.

did.unittitle: Biographical and bibliographic information on Haynes
Commission on Race Relations, Survey Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes for meetings of October 1 and November 12

did.unittitle: Federal Council of Churches, "Towards International Peace"
International Survey of North American Christian Associations
Plenary Meeting of the World's Committee of YMCAs, report
"School of Race Relationship at Camp Kane"
State University of New York Board of Trustees, public hearings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In 1930, Haynes did a survey of the work of the YMCA in South Africa. In 1947 he conducted a similar study in other African nations. This work resulted in Haynes being chosen as consultant on Africa by the World Committee of YMCAs.

did.unittitle: Governmental permits and letters of introduction
"Study Tour in Africa", typescript and handwritten draft, corrected
Dinner given in honor of Dr. Haynes, invitations, letters of regret, and other correspondence
Correspondence, memos, clippings, and other papers
